fault_hypothesis: Line 77 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`now > adjusted_expiry`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= adjusted_expiry`), so when `now` exactly equals `adjusted_expiry` the entry is incorrectly returned as valid instead of expired.

constraints.intent: At the expiry check, when now equals adjusted_expiry the condition must trigger expiry (i.e., >= not just >).
Verify adjusted_expiry is correctly computed so the boundary comparison uses the right value.
Flag the exact-boundary case before the comparison so the strict inequality bug is visible.
After the else branch (entry not expired), now must be strictly less than adjusted_expiry; equality should have gone to the then branch.
